mode_defaults.c(3) Library Functions Manual mode_defaults.c(3)

NAME

mode_defaults.c - Mode-aware default value getters for options.

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct mode_descriptor_t
Metadata for a mode.

Functions


const mode_descriptor_t * get_mode_descriptor (asciichat_mode_t mode)
Get mode descriptor by mode. const mode_descriptor_t * get_modes_by_group (const char *group, size_t *out_count)
Get all mode descriptors grouped by category. const void * get_default_log_file (asciichat_mode_t mode)
Get mode-specific default value for --log-file. const void * get_default_port (asciichat_mode_t mode)
Get mode-specific default value for --port. const void * get_default_websocket_port (asciichat_mode_t mode)
Get mode-specific default value for --websocket-port. void apply_mode_specific_defaults (options_t *opts)
Apply mode-specific defaults to an options struct after mode detection.

Detailed Description

Mode-aware default value getters for options.

Provides mode-specific default values for options that vary by mode, and mode metadata (names, descriptions, groups) for UI generation.

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

February 2026

Definition in file options/registry/mode_defaults.c.

Function Documentation

void apply_mode_specific_defaults (options_t * opts)

Apply mode-specific defaults to an options struct after mode detection. This function updates options that have mode-dependent defaults (port and websocket_port) based on the detected mode. Log file paths are handled separately by options_get_log_filepath().

Parameters

opts Options struct to update (must have detected_mode already set)

Definition at line 192 of file options/registry/mode_defaults.c.

192                                                    {
193   if (!opts) {
194     return;
195   }
196 
197   // Apply port default based on mode
198   const int *mode_port = (const int *)get_default_port(opts->detected_mode);
199   if (mode_port) {
200     // Only update if still at a generic default (27224 or 27225)
201     if (opts->port == OPT_PORT_INT_DEFAULT || opts->port == OPT_ACDS_PORT_INT_DEFAULT) {
202       opts->port = *mode_port;
203     }
204   }
205 
206   // Apply websocket-port default based on mode
207   const int *mode_websocket_port = (const int *)get_default_websocket_port(opts->detected_mode);
208   if (mode_websocket_port) {
209     // Only update if still at a generic default (27226 or 27227)
210     if (opts->websocket_port == OPT_WEBSOCKET_PORT_SERVER_DEFAULT ||
211         opts->websocket_port == OPT_WEBSOCKET_PORT_ACDS_DEFAULT) {
212       opts->websocket_port = *mode_websocket_port;
213     }
214   }
215 }




References get_default_port(), and
    get_default_websocket_port().


Referenced by options_init().






const
  void * get_default_log_file (asciichat_mode_t mode)


Get mode-specific default value for --log-file. Returns empty
    string - the actual log file path is determined by
    options_get_log_filepath() which properly handles debug vs release builds
    and platform-specific temp directories.




Debug builds: Uses current working directory (server.log,
    client.log, etc.) Release builds: Uses $TMPDIR/ascii-chat/server.log,
  etc.


Definition at line 117 of file
    options/registry/mode_defaults.c.


117                                                         {
118   (void)mode; // Unused
119   // Return empty string - let options_get_log_filepath() handle the defaults
120   return "";
121 }










const
  void * get_default_port (asciichat_mode_t mode)


Get mode-specific default value for --port.


Definition at line 135 of file
    options/registry/mode_defaults.c.


135                                                     {
136   switch (mode) {
137   case MODE_SERVER:
138     return &g_port_server;
139   case MODE_CLIENT:
140     return &g_port_client;
141   case MODE_DISCOVERY_SERVICE:
142     return &g_port_discovery_service;
143   case MODE_DISCOVERY:
144     return &g_port_discovery;
145   case MODE_MIRROR:
146   case MODE_INVALID:
147   default:
148     // Fallback
149     return &g_port_server;
150   }
151 }






Referenced by apply_mode_specific_defaults().






const
  void * get_default_websocket_port (asciichat_mode_t mode)


Get mode-specific default value for --websocket-port.


Definition at line 163 of file
    options/registry/mode_defaults.c.


163                                                               {
164   switch (mode) {
165   case MODE_SERVER:
166     return &g_websocket_port_server;
167   case MODE_DISCOVERY_SERVICE:
168     return &g_websocket_port_discovery_service;
169   case MODE_CLIENT:
170   case MODE_MIRROR:
171   case MODE_DISCOVERY:
172   case MODE_INVALID:
173   default:
174     // Fallback (should not be used for client/mirror/discovery)
175     return &g_websocket_port_server;
176   }
177 }






Referenced by apply_mode_specific_defaults().






const mode_descriptor_t * get_mode_descriptor
  (asciichat_mode_t mode)


Get mode descriptor by mode.


Parameters


mode Mode to look up


Returns


Pointer to mode descriptor, or NULL if not found


Definition at line 49 of file
    options/registry/mode_defaults.c.


49                                                                     {
50   for (size_t i = 0; g_mode_descriptors[i].name; i++) {
51     if (g_mode_descriptors[i].mode == mode) {
52       return &g_mode_descriptors[i];
53     }
54   }
55   return NULL;
56 }










const mode_descriptor_t * get_modes_by_group (const char *
  group, size_t * out_count)


Get all mode descriptors grouped by category.


Parameters


group Group name ("server-like" or
  "client-like")


out_count OUTPUT: Number of modes in this group


Returns


Array of mode descriptors, or NULL if group not
  found


Definition at line 64 of file
    options/registry/mode_defaults.c.


64                                                                                   {
65   if (!group || !out_count) {
66     return NULL;
67   }
68 
69   // Count matching modes
70   size_t count = 0;
71   for (size_t i = 0; g_mode_descriptors[i].name; i++) {
72     if (strcmp(g_mode_descriptors[i].group, group) == 0) {
73       count++;
74     }
75   }
76 
77   if (count == 0) {
78     *out_count = 0;
79     return NULL;
80   }
81 
82   // Allocate result array
83   mode_descriptor_t *result = SAFE_MALLOC((count + 1) * sizeof(mode_descriptor_t), mode_descriptor_t *);
84   size_t idx = 0;
85 
86   // Fill result array
87   for (size_t i = 0; g_mode_descriptors[i].name && idx < count; i++) {
88     if (strcmp(g_mode_descriptors[i].group, group) == 0) {
89       result[idx++] = g_mode_descriptors[i];
90     }
91   }
92 
93   // Add terminator
94   result[idx].mode = MODE_INVALID;
95   result[idx].name = NULL;
96   result[idx].description = NULL;
97   result[idx].group = NULL;
98 
99   *out_count = count;
100   return result;
101 }






References mode_descriptor_t::description,
    mode_descriptor_t::group, mode_descriptor_t::mode, and
    mode_descriptor_t::name.


Referenced by completions_generate_zsh().
